does: This paper introduces D3PO (Direct Preference for Denoising Diffusion Policy Optimization), a method for fine-tuning diffusion models using human preference feedback without explicitly training a separate reward model. The approach adapts direct preference optimization from language models to address the memory constraints inherent in diffusion model training, particularly during the denoising process. The authors provide theoretical analysis demonstrating that their method implicitly optimizes an equivalent reward model derived from human preference data, effectively guiding the diffusion model to align with human preferences while eliminating the computational and data overhead of explicitly constructing and training a standalone reward model.
connects: This work builds on the successful application of reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) to diffusion models, which prior work established as a promising direction for preference alignment. The paper directly extends direct preference optimization (DPO), a recent advancement in language model fine-tuning that removes the reward model bottleneck, to the diffusion model domain. It addresses practical limitations that prevented DPO's direct transfer to diffusion models, particularly the prohibitive GPU memory requirements during the iterative denoising process. The research sits within the broader context of preference-based learning and aligns with recent trends in removing intermediate training stages from RLHF pipelines.
enables: D3PO enables more efficient and accessible fine-tuning of diffusion models for preference alignment, reducing the engineering complexity and data requirements compared to traditional RLHF approaches. This opens possibilities for rapid iteration on preference-aligned image and video generation without the bottleneck of reward model training. The method could accelerate research into human-AI alignment for generative models and facilitate deployment of preference-tuned diffusion systems in resource-constrained settings. Future work might explore scaling D3PO to larger diffusion models, combining it with other alignment techniques, or adapting the implicit reward framework to other generative model architectures.
